rationale: The citing paper uses Weiss et al. (2008) in the theory development for the presentness/response-speed construct, specifically to support the claim that quicker responses are associated with higher perceived value of response content. This is not a generic background mention; it is tied to a directional expectation that faster firm responses should improve subsequent user sentiment. The study then operationalizes response time as a focal independent variable and tests its effect, indicating the cited work meaningfully informs the conceptual setup.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Weiss et al.'s insight about response speed and perceived response value to a new setting: firm-customer social media conversations. It uses that prior evidence to justify and test a response-time effect on subsequent user sentiment.
